text: When creating a website, security is essential to protect both user data and the site itself. One fundamental step is using HTTPS, which encrypts data exchanged between a browser and the server. Authentication systems, like strong passwords and two-factor verification, help ensure that only authorized users can access sensitive areas. Additionally, input validation is crucial; developers must check and clean any data submitted through forms to prevent attacks like SQL injection or cross-site scripting. Common vulnerabilities also include outdated software and weak session management. By addressing these areas, developers can significantly reduce risks and build safer, more trustworthy web applications.
